nodes.name: coding-style.md
nodes.summary: React-specific coding-style rules covering file extensions, naming, component shape, JSX conventions, the RSC server/client boundary, imports, hooks discipline, state, and per-component file layout. Extends the TypeScript and common coding-style rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Layered rule file: opens with a blockquote declaring it extends typescript/coding-style.md and common/coding-style.md with React-specific overrides.

nodes.name: hooks.md
nodes.summary: Rules for React hooks — Rules of Hooks, when not to reach for useEffect, dependency arrays, cleanup, when useMemo/useCallback are worth it, custom hooks, useRef/useState/useSyncExternalStore patterns, React 19 additions, and the stale-closure trap. Explicitly distinct from the Claude Code hooks runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: Filename collision-aware: a leading blockquote clarifies this is React hooks, not the repo's hooks/ runtime, and notes it extends typescript/patterns.md and common/patterns.md.

nodes.name: patterns.md
nodes.summary: React architectural patterns: container/presentational split, a state-location decision tree, the RSC server/client boundary, Suspense + error boundaries, forms (controlled/uncontrolled/libraries), data fetching, lists/keys, composition over inheritance, compound components, portals, and React 19 ref forwarding, with pointer sections for Next.js and React Native.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends typescript/patterns.md and common/patterns.md; defers Next.js and React Native specifics to pointer sections and references skills/react-patterns/SKILL.md.

nodes.name: security.md
nodes.summary: React security rules covering XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML, unsafe URL schemes, target=_blank/rel hardening, Server Action input validation, env-var secret exposure, auth, CSP, prototype pollution, SSR template injection, third-party components, and production source-map exposure.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends typescript/security.md and common/security.md; concrete vulnerability-by-vulnerability rules with agent-support guidance.

nodes.name: testing.md
nodes.summary: React testing rules built around Testing Library: library choice, behavior-over-implementation principle, query priority, user-event interaction, async assertions, MSW network mocking, avoiding component snapshots, test setup helpers, custom-hook testing, accessibility assertions, when to reach for Playwright/Cypress, coverage targets, and anti-patterns.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends typescript/testing.md and common/testing.md; references skills/react-testing/SKILL.md for end-to-end scaffolding.
